Tests the Math.atan2 function with NaN as the second argument to ensure it returns NaN.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test script that verifies the behavior of the `Math.atan2` function when one of its arguments is `NaN`. The `assert` function checks if the result of `Math.atan2(1, NaN)` is `NaN`, which is the expected behavior according to the IEEE 754 standard for floating-point arithmetic. This script provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific test case for the `Math.atan2` function. It is useful for ensuring that the JavaScript engine correctly handles `NaN` inputs in trigonometric calculations.
